evals.name: daily-audio-clean-pipecat-logs
evals.prompt:
Customer is reporting that during a PCC session yesterday, the caller 'couldn't hear anything from the bot' for about 30 seconds in the middle of the call, then it recovered. Agent name is voice-support-bot, session ID is b8f2c4d1-3a7e-4f9b-8c62-1d5e7a9b0f34. The transport is Daily WebRTC (the bot runs in a Daily room). I pulled the Pipecat logs already and they look totally clean to me. Here's what I see:

```
2026-04-21T14:30:12.123456Z INFO:     Application startup complete.
2026-04-21T14:30:12.234567Z INFO:     Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:8080 (Press CTRL+C to quit)
2026-04-21T14:30:45.891234Z INFO:     Bot connected to Daily room successfully
2026-04-21T14:31:02.456789Z INFO:     Participant joined: caller-4fde3659
2026-04-21T14:31:03.678901Z INFO:     LLM stream started
2026-04-21T14:34:22.345678Z INFO:     Call ended, cleanup complete
2026-04-21T14:34:22.456789Z INFO:     Application shutdown complete.
2026-04-21T14:34:22.567890Z INFO:     Finished server process [1]
```

What should I look at next?
evals.expected_output: Skill should recognize this matches the Daily /logs follow-up flow: audio complaint + clean Pipecat logs + Daily WebRTC transport. Should explain the session-ID bridge (run `pc cloud agent sessions` to get mtgSessionId from meetingIds) and point at Daily's /logs endpoint with includeMetrics=true, logLevel=ERROR.

evals.name: clear-pipecat-oom-crash
evals.prompt:
My PCC bot keeps crashing after about 2 minutes into calls. Agent is translator-bot. Here's a recent session's logs:

```
2026-04-22T09:11:34.112233Z INFO:     Application startup complete.
2026-04-22T09:11:34.223344Z INFO:     Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:8080
2026-04-22T09:12:01.334455Z INFO:     Participant joined
2026-04-22T09:12:45.445566Z INFO:     LLM streaming started
2026-04-22T09:13:12.556677Z WARNING:  Memory usage at 87% of limit
2026-04-22T09:13:58.667788Z WARNING:  Memory usage at 94% of limit
2026-04-22T09:14:12.778899Z ERROR:    Out of memory
2026-04-22T09:14:12.889900Z ERROR:    Memory cgroup out of memory: Killed process 1 (python3)
```

Session completion status says OOMKilled. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Skill should diagnose this as a clear OOM issue with specific next steps (increase memory limit, find memory leak in the bot code). Should NOT send the user to Daily's /logs endpoint, because the Pipecat agent logs are not clean — they show the crash directly. The Debugging Calls section should be gated out.

evals.name: one-way-audio-pcc-session-id-only
evals.prompt: I got a Plain ticket saying the customer could hear the bot but the bot couldn't hear them on an outbound PSTN call. They gave me the Pipecat Cloud session ID: 800d444a-e67e-4686-afb3-b0de509cccb3, agent name is pstn-concierge. The bot ran through its greeting but then just sat there waiting and eventually hung up. We're using Daily for the WebRTC side of the PSTN bridge. What's the investigation path?
evals.expected_output: Skill should walk through: pull Pipecat logs first to confirm nothing on the agent side failed; if those are clean, bridge from PCC sessionId to Daily mtgSessionId via `pc cloud agent sessions pstn-concierge --id 800d444a-...` reading meetingIds, then hit Daily /logs with includeMetrics=true. Should name DAILY_API_KEY and the mtgSessionId-vs-sessionId distinction.

evals.name: real-session-live-fetch
evals.prompt: I need to debug a PCC session that a customer flagged. Session ID is d1d1723f-94a4-4d95-9f9a-5a282ccb55fa. Can you pull the logs and tell me what happened?
evals.expected_output: Skill should fetch real logs for session d1d1723f-94a4-4d95-9f9a-5a282ccb55fa via `pc cloud agent logs`, resolve the agent name (either from the user, pcc-deploy.toml, or by listing agents), analyze whatever logs come back, consult the Pipecat Docs MCP for any errors found, and present 2-4 ranked root causes with next steps. If the session has no logs (retention window or log-exclusion annotations), explain that clearly rather than making up an analysis.

assertions.text: Identifies the correct owning agent (voiceship) after resolving it without pcc-deploy.toml
Recognizes this as a PSTN dialout to +19169759426 that connected and was answered
Correctly distinguishes 'remote hung up after answering' from the generic 'busy / no answer / remote ended' Daily reason string, using the prior on_dialout_answered event as evidence
Concludes the Pipecat/agent side is healthy (no agent-side bug)
Uses the Pipecat Docs MCP server to inform analysis (not pattern-match alone)
Offers the Daily /logs bridge with mtgSessionId and includeMetrics=true as the concrete follow-up for suspected audio-path issues
Provides concrete, ranked next steps the user can actually take (not generic 'check the logs' advice)
